old: I have once been to that city.
new: I have been to that city before.

old: 这个地区产很多苹果。
new: 这家工厂每年产大量商品。

old: This region produces a lot of apples.
new: This factory produces a large quantity of goods every year.

old: 地球绕太阳转是一门常识。
new: 地球绕太阳转是常识。
reason: 一门常识 unnatural; 门 classifies fields, not individual facts

old: to inherit
new: to bear (a burden, responsibility, or pressure)
reason: 承受 means to bear/endure, not to inherit (that is 继承)

old: 他承受了父亲的事业。
new: 他承受了失去父亲的痛苦。
reason: 承受事业 is wrong; corrected to natural use of 承受 (bearing grief)

old: He inherited his father's business.
new: He bore the pain of losing his father.

old: 她承受了家里的传统。
new: 她独自承受了家里的困难。

old: She inherited the family tradition.
new: She bore the family's hardships alone.

old: 儿子承受了父亲留下的责任。
new: 他承受了巨大的工作压力。
reason: Corrected to clearer 承受 = endure example

old: The son inherited the responsibilities left by his father.
new: He bore enormous work pressure.

old: odoriferous
new: foul-smelling
reason: 臭 means bad/unpleasant smell; 'odoriferous' is neutral-to-positive

old: This flower has a very odoriferous smell.
new: This flower has a very foul smell.

old: This insect emits an odoriferous smell when it senses danger.
new: This insect emits a foul smell when it senses danger.

old: Some fish at the market smell very odoriferous.
new: Some fish at the market smell very foul.

old: 他处置了这件事，很快解决了。
new: 这件事很难处，需要小心。
reason: Sentences use 处置 (different word) instead of headword 处

old: He dealt with this matter and resolved it quickly.
new: This matter is hard to handle and requires care.

old: 这个问题需要认真地处置。
new: 他善于处人际关系。
reason: Replaced 处置 with natural usage of 处 alone

old: This problem needs to be dealt with carefully.
new: He is good at handling interpersonal relationships.

old: 经理亲自处置了这件事情。
new: 这件事情不好处，让我想想。
reason: Replaced 处置 with natural usage of 处 alone

old: The manager personally dealt with this matter.
new: This matter is not easy to handle; let me think about it.

old: 他写了一本关于自己的传。
new: 司马迁写的《史记》中有许多人物的传。
reason: 传 (zhuàn) as standalone noun is unnatural; used naturally in classical history context

old: He wrote a biography about himself.
new: Sima Qian's Records of the Grand Historian contains biographies of many historical figures.

old: 这本书是一位伟人的传。
new: 这篇是李白的传，写得很详细。
reason: More natural use of 传 as classical section title

old: This book is a biography of a great person.
new: This section is the biography of Li Bai, written in great detail.

old: 历史上的名人都有自己的传。
new: 史书里的列传记录了很多名人的故事。
reason: 列传 is the proper compound; 传 standalone as noun is unnatural

old: Historical celebrities all have their own biography.
new: The biographical sections in historical records document the stories of many famous figures.
